what: How does amatrix decide where to run?

code: amatrix_backend_status()[, c("name", "available", "precision_modes", "residency_capable")] #>        name available precision_modes residency_capable #> 1 arrayfire     FALSE            <NA>             FALSE #> 2       cpu      TRUE     strict,fast             FALSE #> 3     metal     FALSE            <NA>             FALSE #> 4       mlx     FALSE            <NA>             FALSE #> 5    opencl     FALSE            <NA>             FALSE plan <- amatrix_backend_plan(X_am, "qr") plan[c("chosen", "chosen_path", "requested_precision")] #> $chosen #> [1] "cpu" #>  #> $chosen_path #> [1] "cold" #>  #> $requested_precision #> [1] "strict"

title: GPU Acceleration: Zero to First Matmul
text: Apple Silicon, yes. Install amatrix.mlx MLX probing default every launch mode – interactive, Rscript -e, Rscript file.R. first time session needs GPU, amatrix runs one-shot probe disposable child process (bad driver crashes child, never session) routes eligible work MLX automatically. nothing call. see one-line note package attaches, confirming available: line suppressible usual way suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(amatrix)), automatic probe can turned options(amatrix.auto_probe = FALSE) environment variable AMATRIX_MLX_PROBE_GPU=0. permanently quiet attach, set options(amatrix.quiet_startup = TRUE) .Rprofile (export AMATRIX_QUIET=1); options(amatrix.optional_backends = FALSE) goes turns optional GPU backends entirely, per-backend options(amatrix.disable_mlx = TRUE) drops just one. None affects correctness — note purely informational attaching package GPU probing. Everywhere else – OpenCL, ArrayFire, Metal – backends opt-, turn one call. amatrix_use_gpu() walks preferred order, enables health-probes first installed backend, sets fast-precision default, prints one confirmation line: amatrix_use_gpu() works Apple Silicon want explicit confirmation line, pass backend = "mlx" force specific one. returns enabled backend name invisibly, FALSE none brought .
code: library(amatrix) #> amatrix GPU backends: mlx ready (activates on first use). See amatrix_gpu_status(). amatrix_use_gpu() #> amatrix: GPU enabled - opencl backend (float32 'fast' precision, ~1e-4 vs #> float64; 'strict' float64 stays on CPU). amatrix_gpu_status() for details.

what: Am I actually on the GPU?
title: GPU Acceleration: Zero to First Matmul
text: Two functions answer . amatrix_gpu_status() “() GPU?” table – one row per backend, walking every gate installed computing device: Read left right: backend installed, registered, report available, pass health probe work lands . reason column names first gate failed – “package installed”, “opt-backend; enable amatrix_use_gpu()”, driver-specific message probe. single operation, amatrix_explain() shows actual dispatch decision: backend chosen, every candidate accepted rejected, . mode = "fast" constructor makes object eligible GPU – next. amatrix_explain() returns underlying plan invisibly; amatrix_backend_plan(X, "matmul") gives decision plain list want test code.
code: amatrix_gpu_status() #>     backend       package installed registered available  health #> 1       mlx   amatrix.mlx      TRUE       TRUE      TRUE healthy #> 2     metal amatrix.metal     FALSE      FALSE     FALSE unprobed #> 3 arrayfire amatrix.arr..     FALSE      FALSE     FALSE unprobed #> 4    opencl amatrix.opencl    FALSE      FALSE     FALSE unprobed #>                                       reason #> 1                                       <NA> #> 2              package amatrix.metal not installed #> 3          package amatrix.arrayfire not installed #> 4             package amatrix.opencl not installed X <- adgeMatrix(matrix(rnorm(4096 * 4096), 4096), mode = "fast") amatrix_explain(X, "matmul") #> ── amatrix dispatch: matmul ───────────────────────────────────────── #>   object:    adgeMatrix [4096 × 4096]  precision=fast  preferred=mlx #>   residency: host (not GPU-resident) #> #> ── candidates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>   ► CHOSEN   mlx            reg avail prec cold calib  [cold] #>     ......   cpu            reg avail prec cold calib #> #> ── result ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>   chosen: mlx  via cold path (upload + compute) #> ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

what: What happens when the GPU fails?
title: GPU Acceleration: Zero to First Matmul
text: falls back CPU keeps going. Every runtime GPU failure degrades CPU reference path, marks backend’s health, records entry can inspect: non-empty fallback log means backend claimed op actually execute – worth reporting, results still correct CPU produced . Clear amatrix_fallback_log_reset() want isolate fresh run. severe failure – crash first-use probe – contained child process disables backend rest session actionable reason. see amatrix_gpu_status(): troubleshooting loop always : read reason column. routes fix – install package, call amatrix_use_gpu(), set probe env var, use bigger matrix, file driver crash.

what: The one sharp edge: resident handles alias the GPU buffer
title: GPU Acceleration: Zero to First Matmul
text: Ordinary amatrix objects copy--write safe, exactly like base R matrices. Assigning modifying copy never touches original: resident_handle() deliberate exception. mutable wrapper used iterative algorithms avoid per-step object allocation, -place operators (am_sweep_inplace(), am_ewise_inplace()) mutate device buffer directly. build handle source already GPU-resident, handle reuses buffer rather re-uploading – -place op handle also mutates source object aliases pointing buffer: intentional: sharing buffer whole point resident handle hot path. want independent copy mutate, build handle non-resident source (plain matrix, adgeMatrix bound resident), case resident_handle() uploads fresh buffer owns exclusively. doubt, keep -place handle work objects still intend read.
code: Y <- X Y[1, 1] <- 999 X[1, 1]        # unchanged #> [1] 0.418 X <- adgeMatrix(A, mode = "fast") X <- amatrix_bind_resident(X, "mlx")   # X now owns a resident buffer  h <- resident_handle(X)                # reuses X's buffer -- shared, not copied am_ewise_inplace(h, 2, "*")            # doubles the buffer in place  # X sees the change too -- reference semantics, by design as.matrix(X)[1, 1] == 2 * A[1, 1] #> [1] TRUE

title: When is amatrix fast?
text: smallest largest, ’s actually happens call op adgeMatrix: Small matrices (roughly < 1000 elements). Upload latency alone dwarfs compute. dispatcher routes CPU. 32×32 matmul MLX slower base BLAS, every time. Medium matrices (10³–10⁵ elements). Depends op. Dense matmul factorizations start winning GPU around 512×512. Per-row reductions (rowSums, colSums) may still CPU-bound ’re memory-bound, compute-bound. Large matrices (> 10⁵ elements, dense). GPU wins decisively %*%, crossprod, chol, svd, qr. Factor caching (chol_factor, svd_factor) amplifies : first call pays upload + factorize cost, subsequent calls reuse cached factor memory speed. large matrices (memory-pressure regime, 4K×4K+). baseline tracks explicitly cache effects dominate. CPU’s speedup-vs-GPU ratio gets worse n grows. Iterative algorithms (rsvd, block_lanczos, sinkhorn, irlba). many sequential kernel invocations resident data. input already GPU-resident, amortize upload cost across hundreds kernel launches. input cold every time, ’re paying upload .

what: When the CPU wins (and you should let it)
title: When is amatrix fast?
text: 1×1, 1×n, n×1 matrices. Always CPU. Degenerate shapes never route GPU backend; package’s adversarial-input test suite pins behavior. Element-wise ops tiny matrices. dispatch overhead exceeds compute cost. Ops allocate compute. GPU spends time copying bytes running flops, CPU wins. Backends don’t . Optional backends (amatrix.mlx, amatrix.arrayfire, amatrix.opencl, amatrix.metal) separate packages; ’re installed, dispatcher routes CPU. health probe amatrix_backend_status() tells ’s registered backends passed canary check.

title: Lazy Kronecker product of two matrices — KronMatrix-class
text: KronMatrix stores two factor matrices (m x n) B (p x q) without forming full (mp x nq) Kronecker product. Matrix-vector matrix-matrix products evaluated using vec-permutation identity (x B) vec(X) = vec(B X t()), keeping memory use O(mn + pq) rather O(mnpq).

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: Initial public release.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: S4 classes adgeMatrix (dense) adgCMatrix (sparse) extend Matrix package backend-dispatch slots transparent GPU acceleration. Five backend targets: CPU (default), MLX (Apple Silicon), OpenCL (CLBlast), Metal (Apple sparse), ArrayFire (portable GPU). Three precision modes: "strict" (float64 CPU), "exact" (float64 CPU-pinned), "fast" (float32-permitted GPU). Automatic dispatch planning calibration thresholds, resident memory management, predictable CPU fallback.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: matmul, crossprod, tcrossprod, covariance, dist_matrix — backend-dispatched GPU-resident fast paths. chol_factor, lu_factor, svd_factor — factorization caching solve, project, reconstruct methods. eigh, am_svd, rsvd, block_lanczos — eigendecomposition randomized/iterative spectral methods. sinkhorn — doubly-stochastic scaling GPU-resident key-chain loop. ewise, am_sweep, segment_sum, segment_mean — element-wise reduction operations. woodbury_solve, woodbury_logdet — Woodbury matrix identity solvers.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: many_lm — fit multiple linear models via GPU-accelerated QR coefficient caching. lm_fit, array_lm, wls_fit — single weighted least squares. ridge_path, ridge_fit — ridge regression lambda grids. lm_loo_cv — leave-one-cross-validation via QR downdate. pca_coef — PCA coefficient extraction cached SVD factors.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: adgCMatrix backend-dispatched SpMV SpMM via OpenCL, Metal, MLX resident paths. block_lanczos svd_factor subspace iteration sparse inputs.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: amatrix.mlx — Apple MLX backend via mlx-c; full dense + sparse support. amatrix.opencl — OpenCL + CLBlast backend; dense BLAS, factorizations, sparse products. amatrix.metal — Apple Metal backend; sparse-first MPSGraph kernels. amatrix.arrayfire — ArrayFire backend; portable GPU safety gates Apple Silicon.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: tools/benchmark-regression.R — canonical regression harness per-backend worker isolation, direct-file source-entry parity, automatic baseline comparison.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: amatrix_backend_health_probe() — run canary op backend record health (healthy / unhealthy:<reason>). Every backend probed first use; subsequent routing decisions respect probe. amatrix_backend_status() now reports per-backend health health_reason columns alongside capabilities, features, precision modes. amatrix_fallback_log() amatrix_fallback_log_reset() — structured log every dispatch fall-backend CPU. non-empty log clean conformance run stop-ship condition. amatrix_benchmark_report() — reads tools/baseline.csv cached calibration, returning cold-vs-warm timings, warm/cold ratios, calibrated thresholds, gpu_wins flags per (op × backend). Calibration cache tagged sys_hash OS, machine, platform, R version. Stale caches different machine rejected automatically instead producing wrong routing. Auto-fallback telemetry: every backend error amatrix_dispatch_op re-signaled calling-style condition (withCallingHandlers can observe original error class) emits amatrix_fallback condition structured metadata, routing CPU. mode = "balanced" now deprecated. never fully implemented (routed CPU hood) now maps "exact" one-time-per-session deprecation warning. Use "exact" "fast".

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: User-facing errors wrapper functions now emit classed conditions (amatrix_bad_arg, amatrix_bad_backend, amatrix_backend_exists, amatrix_subspace_error) call = NULL messages don’t leak internal call stacks. tryCatch(..., amatrix_bad_arg = ...) expect_error(class = "amatrix_bad_arg") work.

title: amatrix 0.1.0
text: planning_docs/quality-tracking.md authoritative quality doc. carries coverage matrix (every exported op × four test types + benchmark row + per-backend tier), stop-ship rules, honest backend tier assessment. tools/audit-dispatch.R — static AST-based audit enumerates every required dispatch signature %*% / crossprod / tcrossprod mixed plain/amatrix pairs fails PR gate missing. Residency tripwire (options(amatrix.residency.tripwire = TRUE) AMATRIX_RESIDENCY_TRIPWIRE=1) increments counter every real GPU--host copy; conformance suite asserts zero trips clean run. Three-lane CI: PR gate (.github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml + fast-gates job), nightly stress (nightly-stress.yaml), release gate (release-gate.yaml required reviewers environment).
